title: carex oederi subsp. oedocarpa
intro: Carex oederi subsp. oedocarpa, also known as Oeder's sedge, is a type of perennial plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae, characterized by its distinctive yellow-green leaves and brownish-red inflorescences. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help to soothe and protect the skin. The health benefits of Carex oederi subsp. oedocarpa include its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its use in traditional remedies for fever and respiratory issues. Carex oederi subsp. oedocarpa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves, and is often used in combination with other herbs in traditional folk medicine practices.
